questions.question: A developer is starting a new application that will be distributed to multiple customer instances via the ServiceNow Store. Which application scope should be used?
options: Global scope, so any user can extend the app's tables
A private scope with a unique scope name (e.g., x_acme_pet)
The 'public' scope reserved for store-distributed apps
It does not matter — scope only affects performance, not distribution
questions.explanation: Store-distributed applications must use a private scope, which gives the app a unique namespace prefix (e.g., x_acme_pet) and isolates its artifacts from other applications. Global scope cannot be installed cleanly into customer instances and offers no protection of intellectual property. There is no special 'public' scope.

questions.question: When deciding whether a business problem is a good fit for a ServiceNow custom application, which TWO factors are the STRONGEST positive indicators?
options: The process involves structured data, workflows, and roles that map to the platform's task model
The solution needs millisecond-latency real-time streaming with sub-second SLAs
Users already work in ServiceNow and would benefit from a unified experience
The data set is hundreds of terabytes of unstructured media files
questions.explanation: ServiceNow excels at structured, task-oriented processes and benefits from users already living in the platform. Real-time streaming and very large unstructured media stores are anti-patterns — the platform isn't optimized for those workloads and you'd be fighting the architecture.

questions.question: You need a new table 'Pet' that should inherit fields and behaviors from Task (assignment, state, work notes). What is the correct way to model this?
options: Create a standalone table and copy the Task fields manually
Extend the Task table when creating the Pet table
Add a Reference field on Pet that points to Task
Create a Database View joining Pet and Task
questions.explanation: Extending Task is the canonical pattern — the new table inherits all Task fields, business rules, and the standard work-management UI. Manual copying duplicates structure but not behavior; a Reference field models association, not inheritance; a Database View is read-only and for reporting.

questions.question: In Studio, which artifact is created automatically when you add a new table to an application and is responsible for grouping the table's records in the left-nav navigator?
options: An Application Menu and a Module
A UI Page and a UI Macro
A Form Section and a Related List
A Catalog Item and a Variable Set
questions.explanation: Studio prompts you to create an Application Menu (the category in the navigator) and one or more Modules (the clickable links that filter records). Form Sections, UI Pages, and Catalog Items are unrelated to the navigator structure.

questions.question: An application table has a Reference field pointing to sys_user. Which configuration setting on that field BEST limits the reference picker to only active users in a specific department?
options: Add a Business Rule on sys_user that hides inactive users
Set a Reference Qualifier (simple or advanced) on the field
Add an Access Control on the sys_user table
Use a Data Policy on the application table
questions.explanation: Reference Qualifiers are the dedicated mechanism for narrowing what records appear in a reference picker, supporting both simple (encoded query) and advanced (script) forms. Business Rules on sys_user would affect all consumers, ACLs control access not picker visibility, and Data Policies enforce values but don't filter reference choices.

questions.question: Which statement about Application Files in Studio is TRUE?
options: They are physical files on the server filesystem outside the database
They are records in sys_metadata that belong to the application and travel with it on install/upgrade
They are only visible to administrators in the global scope
They are equivalent to attachments on the Application record
questions.explanation: Every artifact in ServiceNow (Business Rules, Tables, UI Actions, Script Includes, etc.) is stored as a record extending sys_metadata. Application Files are the collection of those records associated with an app's scope and are what gets packaged for distribution. They are not OS files or attachments.

questions.question: You are designing a 1-to-many relationship: one Pet Owner can have many Pets. Where should the Reference field be placed?
options: On the Pet Owner table, referencing Pet
On the Pet table, referencing Pet Owner
On a separate junction table joining both
On both tables, in opposite directions
questions.explanation: In a 1-to-many relationship, the Reference field always lives on the 'many' side pointing to the 'one' side — each Pet record stores a single owner. Putting it on Pet Owner would model 1-to-1; junction tables are for many-to-many; placing it on both creates a redundant cycle.

questions.question: Which TWO of the following are valid uses of Update Sets in a development lifecycle?
options: Capturing changes made in a dev instance to promote to test or prod
Packaging an application for distribution via the ServiceNow Store
Tracking customizations to global-scope artifacts
Backing up the user's personal preferences
questions.explanation: Update Sets capture in-flight customizations (especially in global scope) for promotion between instances. Scoped applications are distributed via the Application Repository or Store, not Update Sets. User preferences are not metadata and are not captured.

questions.question: Which feature lets a scoped application explicitly allow OTHER applications to read its tables, scripts, or APIs?
options: Cross-Scope Access Privileges (sys_scope_privilege)
Application Menu Roles
Reference Qualifiers
Data Policies
questions.explanation: Cross-scope access privileges are the explicit allow-list a scoped app uses to expose resources to other scopes. Application Menu Roles only control navigator visibility; Reference Qualifiers and Data Policies have no security role for cross-scope access.

questions.question: A field uses 'Choice' type with hard-coded values defined on the dictionary entry. The developer wants the choices to differ by record (dependent choices). What is the correct approach?
options: Add an onLoad Client Script that rewrites the choices each time
Mark the field as 'Dependent' and set a Dependent Field reference
Use a Reference field instead and filter with a Reference Qualifier
Either B or C is acceptable depending on whether the choices are static or data-driven
questions.explanation: Dependent Field is the platform mechanism for static choice subsets driven by another field's value; a Reference field with a qualifier is appropriate when the choice set is itself a table of records. Both are valid — the choice depends on whether the source of values is static or data-driven. A Client Script rewriting choices is fragile and not the standard pattern.

questions.question: What does the 'Auto Number' configuration on a table provide?
options: A unique sys_id for each record
A human-readable, sequential number on records (e.g., PET0001001) stored on a configurable field
Automatic copying of the parent table's number
A locked database sequence visible only to admins
questions.explanation: Auto Number provides a configurable, human-readable sequential identifier (prefix, counter, length) typically stored on a 'number' field. sys_id is a separate 32-character GUID created automatically for every record regardless of auto-number configuration.

questions.question: When extending a table, which behavior is INHERITED by the child table?
options: Only fields — business rules and ACLs must be redefined
Fields, business rules, client scripts, and ACLs defined on the parent
Only ACLs — the parent's logic is intentionally isolated
Nothing — 'extending' is purely a naming convention
questions.explanation: Child tables inherit fields, business rules, client scripts, ACLs, and other platform behaviors from their parent. This is why extending Task gives you the entire task lifecycle for free. The child can override or add to inherited behavior but cannot opt out of it wholesale.

questions.question: Which client-script type runs only when the user attempts to save the form?
options: onLoad
onChange
onSubmit
onCellEdit
questions.explanation: onSubmit runs at form submission and can cancel the save by returning false. onLoad runs once when the form is rendered, onChange fires whenever a watched field's value changes, and onCellEdit fires during inline list editing.

questions.question: A UI Policy and a Client Script both manipulate the visibility of the same field. The UI Policy is configured with 'On load' set to true and 'Reverse if false' set to true. Which behavior wins on form load?
options: Whichever runs last — typically the Client Script
The UI Policy, because it has higher order precedence than scripts
Both run; the field's final state depends on script ordering and the policy's Order field
Neither runs on load — UI Policies only run on change
questions.explanation: UI Policies and Client Scripts both execute, and the final state depends on execution ordering (Order field on each artifact) and any explicit code overrides. UI Policies do run on load when 'On load' is true. There is no global rule that policies win over scripts.

questions.question: Which API on the client form object hides the field 'description' from the current form?
options: g_form.setDisplay('description', false)
g_form.hideField('description')
g_user.hide('description')
current.description.setVisible(false)
questions.explanation: g_form.setDisplay(fieldName, boolean) is the canonical API for showing/hiding fields on the client. setVisible was deprecated/removed; hideField is not a real method; g_user is for user information, not form manipulation; 'current' is server-side syntax.

questions.question: Which UI artifact embeds inside a form (e.g., next to a field) to display rich custom HTML without affecting data, such as a calculated KPI summary?
options: UI Action
UI Macro (rendered as a Formatter)
UI Page
Related List
questions.explanation: A UI Macro rendered via a Formatter embeds custom Jelly/HTML inside a form without storing data — the typical pattern for read-only display content like KPIs. UI Actions are buttons/links; UI Pages are standalone full pages; Related Lists show related records, not arbitrary HTML.

questions.question: An onChange Client Script needs to call a Script Include to compute a value. Which API should it use to invoke the Script Include asynchronously?
options: GlideRecord on the client
GlideAjax
g_form.callScriptInclude()
RESTMessageV2
questions.explanation: GlideAjax is the canonical mechanism for the client to call a Script Include (which must extend AbstractAjaxProcessor). GlideRecord is server-side; g_form.callScriptInclude is not a real API; RESTMessageV2 is for outbound integrations from the server.

questions.question: Which TWO statements about UI Actions are TRUE?
options: They can be configured to appear as form buttons, links, list buttons, or context-menu items
They can execute either client-side or server-side script depending on the 'Client' checkbox
They run only after a Business Rule sequence completes
They cannot be restricted by role
questions.explanation: UI Actions are versatile — they render as buttons, links, list actions, or context menu items, and they support a client-side script (Onclick, with 'Client' true), a server-side script, or both via the 'Action name' pattern. They can absolutely be restricted by role on the UI Action record itself; they do not require a Business Rule to run.

questions.question: What is the correct way to mark a field as mandatory at the FORM level (for one specific form/view), without affecting other forms or the dictionary?
options: Set 'Mandatory' to true on the dictionary entry
Use a UI Policy with the field condition and the 'Mandatory' action
Add a Business Rule that aborts saves when the field is empty
Set the field's default value to '*'
questions.explanation: UI Policies allow per-view, per-form mandatory configuration without altering the dictionary. Dictionary 'Mandatory' applies globally to every form and API path. Business Rules abort saves but don't change the UI affordance. Default values are unrelated.

questions.question: Which Jelly tag in a UI Macro outputs a value while ESCAPING HTML to prevent XSS?
options: <j:set>
<g:evaluate>
questions.explanation: Standard ${expression} interpolation in Jelly performs HTML-escaping by default. $[expression] outputs the value WITHOUT escaping (use only when you know the value is safe). j:set sets a variable, and g:evaluate runs script blocks.

questions.question: When designing a Service Portal widget instead of a classic UI Page, which client-side API takes the place of g_form?
options: $scope or this within the widget's client controller
g_user
current
GlideRecord on the client
questions.explanation: Service Portal widgets use AngularJS-style client controllers; you bind state to $scope (or 'this' / 'c' in modern syntax) and communicate with the server via the widget's server script. g_form is a classic-UI artifact and is not present in the portal.

questions.question: What is the recommended way to add a sortable, filterable table of related records to a form?
options: Insert a Related List on the form view
Write a UI Macro that renders an HTML table
Use a UI Page in an iframe
Add a Glide List field
questions.explanation: Related Lists are the platform's first-class affordance for displaying related records with sorting, filtering, paging, and inline editing. UI Macros and UI Pages would reimplement what Related Lists already do; Glide List is a many-to-many storage field, not a display widget.

questions.question: Which g_form method returns the underlying value of a Reference field (the sys_id), not its display value?
options: g_form.getValue('owner')
g_form.getDisplayValue('owner')
g_form.getReference('owner')
g_form.getLabel('owner')
questions.explanation: g_form.getValue returns the raw stored value, which for Reference fields is the sys_id. getDisplayValue returns the human-readable string; getReference fetches the referenced GlideRecord asynchronously; getLabel returns the field's label text.

questions.question: A developer wants the SAME visibility logic enforced both in the UI and on direct REST/API writes. Which mechanism enforces both?
options: UI Policy
Client Script
Data Policy
Formatter
questions.explanation: Data Policies enforce mandatory/read-only at both the UI and API/import layers, making them the right tool when business rules must apply regardless of how data enters the system. UI Policies and Client Scripts run in the browser only. Formatters are display-only.

questions.question: Which automation tool is RECOMMENDED for net-new processes on current ServiceNow releases?
options: Classic Workflow (Workflow Editor)
Flow Designer
Graphical Workflow with embedded Glide scripts
Approval Engine
questions.explanation: Flow Designer is the strategic, no-/low-code automation builder for current releases. Classic Workflow is being phased out and exists primarily for backward compatibility. 'Approval Engine' is not a separate product — it's a component used inside flows/workflows.

questions.question: A scheduled job needs to run a server script every weeknight at 02:00. Which artifact configures this?
options: A Business Rule with 'when' set to 'async'
A Scheduled Script Execution (sysauto_script)
An Event Registration
A Flow Designer Schedule trigger
questions.explanation: Scheduled Script Executions are the dedicated record type for time-based server-side jobs with cron-like scheduling. Business Rules are event-driven, not time-driven; Event Registrations declare event types; Flow Designer's Schedule trigger is also valid but the question specifies a server script, which is the Scheduled Script Execution pattern.

questions.question: What does gs.eventQueue() do?
options: Immediately processes the named event in the current transaction
Inserts a row in sysevent for asynchronous processing by Script Actions and Notifications
Cancels any pending event of the named type
Subscribes the current user to push notifications for that event
questions.explanation: gs.eventQueue(name, record, parm1, parm2) enqueues an event in sysevent for asynchronous handling by Script Actions, Notifications, and Workflow event triggers. Events are not processed inline by eventQueue; cancellation and push subscriptions are not part of its contract.

questions.question: An Email Notification should send when an Incident is closed AND its priority was Critical. Which TWO configurations achieve this?
options: Set 'When to send' to 'Record inserted or updated' with condition: state CHANGES TO Closed AND priority IS 1
Trigger via an event (e.g., incident.closed.critical) raised from a Business Rule when the conditions are met
Add a UI Policy that emails on form close
Hard-code the email body in a Script Include
questions.explanation: Both are valid — a condition-based notification works directly off record state, and an event-driven pattern decouples detection (Business Rule) from delivery (notification listening for the event). UI Policies don't send email; Script Includes are libraries, not delivery mechanisms.

questions.question: Inbound Email Actions process incoming email by matching a regex on the subject and either creating or updating a record. Where is the body of the inbound email accessible in the action's script?
options: email.body_text and email.body_html
current.body
g_form.getMessage()
gs.email.read()
questions.explanation: The 'email' object exposes body_text, body_html, subject, from, headers, and other fields inside Inbound Email Action scripts. current refers to the record being updated/created; g_form is client-only; gs.email.read is not a real API.

questions.question: Which THREE trigger types are supported in Flow Designer?
options: Record (created/updated) trigger
Schedule (daily, weekly, monthly, custom)
Mouse-click trigger that fires on any browser event
Inbound Email trigger
questions.explanation: Flow Designer supports Record triggers, Schedule triggers, Inbound Email triggers, REST triggers, and several others. There is no mouse-click trigger in Flow Designer — browser events are the domain of Client Scripts.

questions.question: Which Flow Designer artifact is BEST suited when you want to encapsulate a reusable set of actions that returns outputs and can be called from multiple flows?
options: Subflow
Custom Action
Decision Table
Script Action
questions.explanation: Subflows are reusable, parameterized chunks of automation that accept inputs and return outputs and are called by other flows or subflows. Custom Actions are also reusable but represent a single step (often wrapping a script or integration), not a sequence; Decision Tables route based on conditions; Script Actions are event-handlers.

questions.question: What is the maximum recommended approach for running long-running jobs that could exceed the standard transaction limit?
options: Wrap the work in a single Business Rule and increase the timeout
Move the work to a Scheduled Script Execution or an asynchronous Business Rule and process in batches
Run the work in an onSubmit Client Script so it runs in the user's browser
Use a UI Macro with embedded GlideAjax calls in a loop
questions.explanation: Long-running work belongs in async processing (Scheduled jobs or async Business Rules) ideally in batches to stay within transaction limits and avoid blocking UI threads. Increasing timeouts is fragile; client-side loops degrade UX; UI Macros are display artifacts.

questions.question: Which Business Rule 'when' setting runs the script AFTER the record is written to the database, suitable for side effects like creating related records?
options: before
after
async
display
questions.explanation: 'after' Business Rules run after the database write but within the same transaction — ideal for synchronous side effects that depend on the saved sys_id. 'before' runs before the write (use for validation or in-memory mutation); 'async' runs in a background queue; 'display' runs on form load to populate scratchpad variables.

questions.question: A nightly job ingests a CSV from an SFTP server into a staging table, then promotes rows to the target table with field-level transforms. Which TWO ServiceNow artifacts model this?
options: Data Source
Transform Map
Database View
GlideAjax
questions.explanation: Data Sources define connections and ingest data into Import Sets (staging tables); Transform Maps move and shape data from the import set into the target table with field mapping and scripted transforms. Database Views are read-only joins; GlideAjax is a client/server bridge.

questions.question: Which API constructs an outbound REST call to a third-party endpoint and returns the response synchronously?
options: RESTMessageV2 with execute()
RESTMessageV2 with executeAsync()
GlideHTTPRequest
GlideAjax
questions.explanation: RESTMessageV2.execute() returns a response synchronously; executeAsync() returns immediately with a tracking sys_id and runs in the background. GlideHTTPRequest is a deprecated lower-level API; GlideAjax is client-to-server, not outbound.

questions.question: A Transform Map needs to update existing target records when the import-set row matches on 'Email', otherwise insert a new one. Which configuration achieves this?
options: Set the Email field mapping's 'Coalesce' checkbox
Add an onBefore script that calls update() or insert()
Mark the Transform Map as 'Run business rules' false
Set the target table's auto-number prefix
questions.explanation: The Coalesce flag on one or more field mappings tells the transform to match on those fields — if a match exists, update; otherwise, insert. Scripted insert/update inside transforms works but isn't the declarative pattern. The other options are unrelated to upsert semantics.

questions.question: Which inbound integration mechanism lets external systems POST JSON to a custom URL and run developer-defined script to handle the request?
options: Scripted REST API
SOAP Message
Data Source
Web Service Import
questions.explanation: Scripted REST APIs expose configurable resources at /api/<namespace>/<name>/<resource> with full script control over request handling. SOAP Messages are outbound legacy SOAP; Data Sources are inbound bulk ingestion; Web Service Imports are auto-generated table-based endpoints, not custom logic.

questions.question: When configuring an outbound REST Message with OAuth 2.0, which artifact stores the client id, client secret, token endpoint, and scopes?
options: An OAuth Application Registry record
A system property under 'glide.outbound.oauth'
An attribute on the REST Message itself, encrypted at rest
A Script Include named OAuthHelper
questions.explanation: OAuth Application Registry entries hold the client id, secret, token URL, refresh URL, scopes, and grant type. The REST Message references this profile to obtain tokens at runtime. Properties, in-line attributes, and ad-hoc script libraries are not the standard mechanism.

questions.question: An Import Set has 100k rows but the developer only wants to test the transform on the first 10. What is the BEST approach?
options: Truncate the import set table manually
Set the Data Source to load 'Test load 20 records', then run the transform on that small set
Edit the Transform Map to LIMIT 10
Delete the production rows and re-import
questions.explanation: Data Sources expose a 'Test load' action that pulls a small sample for safe end-to-end validation. Manually truncating is destructive, Transform Maps don't have row limits, and deleting production data is obviously unsafe.

questions.question: An ACL evaluates THREE elements (when all are configured) before granting access. Which list is correct?
options: Roles, Conditions, and Script (advanced)
Roles, UI Policy, and Client Script
Roles, Field Encryption, and Audit
Conditions, Domain Separation, and Transform
questions.explanation: An ACL grants access only when the user has at least one of the required roles, the condition evaluates true, and the optional advanced script returns true. UI Policies, Client Scripts, encryption, audit, and domain separation are separate mechanisms.

questions.question: Which ACL operation governs whether a user can SEE a record in a list or form?
options: read
write
create
delete
questions.explanation: The 'read' operation gates visibility — if a user fails the read ACL, the record is filtered out of lists and forms. 'write' controls modification, 'create' controls insertion, and 'delete' controls removal.

questions.question: Both a table-level ACL and a field-level ACL exist. Both must pass for which combination?
options: Listing the record — only table.read matters
Reading a specific field on the form — BOTH table.read AND table.field.read are evaluated
Editing the record — only the field-level write ACL matters
Deleting the record — BOTH table and field ACLs must pass
questions.explanation: ServiceNow evaluates ACLs hierarchically: to access a specific field, the user must first pass the table-level operation (e.g., table.read) and then the field-level operation (table.field.read). For delete and list operations, only the table-level ACL applies.

questions.question: A custom role 'x_acme_pet.shop_owner' inherits from 'itil'. Which TWO consequences follow?
options: Users granted x_acme_pet.shop_owner automatically have all access granted by itil
The role contains its parent's role grants via the sys_user_role_contains table
Users granted itil automatically get x_acme_pet.shop_owner as well
Inheritance only applies to UI navigation, not ACL evaluation
questions.explanation: Role inheritance is unidirectional — child roles inherit parent roles, recorded in sys_user_role_contains. Granting the parent does NOT grant the child. ACL evaluation fully respects inheritance, including all child grants.

questions.question: An external scoped app needs to write to a table in your scoped app. Which configuration MUST be in place on your side?
options: Mark the table 'Public' on the dictionary
Add a sys_scope_privilege record granting Write access from the consuming scope to your table
Disable ACLs on the table
Give the calling user the admin role
questions.explanation: Cross-scope writes require an explicit sys_scope_privilege entry that allows the consuming scope to write the target artifact. Marking tables 'Public' is not a real configuration. Disabling ACLs and granting admin are heavy-handed and don't replace scope privileges.

questions.question: Which API checks whether the current user has a specific role from server-side script?
options: gs.hasRole('itil')
g_user.hasRole('itil')
current.hasRole('itil')
GlideUser.role('itil')
questions.explanation: gs.hasRole(name) is the server-side check (returns true also for admin). g_user.hasRole exists on the client. current is the current record, not a user. GlideUser.role is not a real API.

questions.question: A Business Rule contains gs.getUser().getRecord(). The rule must enforce that the calling user has the 'x_acme_pet.manager' role. What is the SAFEST way to enforce this?
options: Trust the role grants — if the user got this far, they have it
Wrap the work in if (gs.hasRole('x_acme_pet.manager')) { ... } and fall through to gs.addErrorMessage otherwise
Use a Client Script to hide the trigger UI from non-managers
Add an ACL on the table
questions.explanation: Server-side checks (gs.hasRole) are the only authoritative enforcement — client-side hides are advisory only and ACLs may not cover every path that triggers the rule. Trusting upstream grants is dangerous when business rules can run via async paths or scripted invocations.

questions.question: Which TWO of the following help prevent a developer from inadvertently exposing sensitive data via a custom UI Page or Service Portal widget?
options: Always render values with ${...} (escaped) rather than $[...] (raw)
Apply read ACLs on the source table and field
Disable HTTPS during development for clearer error messages
Store secrets in client-side cookies for easy retrieval
questions.explanation: Escaped interpolation prevents XSS injection of attacker-controlled content; ACLs ensure users only see what they're authorized to. Disabling HTTPS and storing secrets in cookies are both serious anti-patterns that EXPAND the attack surface.

questions.question: What does the 'Admin overrides' checkbox on an ACL control?
options: Whether the admin role can bypass the ACL even if the other elements fail
Whether the ACL is editable only by admins
Whether the ACL runs before all other ACLs
Whether the script element overrides the condition
questions.explanation: When 'Admin overrides' is true (default), the admin role bypasses the ACL automatically. Unchecking it forces even admins to satisfy the ACL's roles/conditions/script — used for hardened ACLs like security event tables.

questions.question: Which TWO mechanisms are used to distribute a scoped application from a development instance to a production instance owned by the same company?
options: Publish to the company's Application Repository, then install on the target instance
Export the application as an Update Set XML and load it
Email the scope directory as a ZIP
Copy rows directly from sys_metadata via a JDBC query
questions.explanation: Application Repository (internal store) is the modern path; legacy Update Set export/import still works for one-off transfers. Emailing ZIPs and direct database copying are not supported and bypass version tracking.

questions.question: What does the application 'Version' field control?
options: The release version of ServiceNow the app requires
The semantic version of the application itself, used by the repository for upgrade detection
The number of users licensed for the app
The minimum browser version supported
questions.explanation: The Version field is the app's own semantic version (e.g., 1.2.3) used by the Application Repository to decide whether an installed app is up-to-date and to drive upgrades. ServiceNow release dependencies are tracked separately. The field has no licensing or browser role.

questions.question: A developer is configuring the Automated Test Framework (ATF). Which TWO statements about ATF are TRUE?
options: Tests can simulate user actions on forms and lists, including impersonation
Tests can run as part of an Update Set promotion to validate changes
ATF replaces unit tests written in Mocha or Jest in the codebase
ATF only works on the platform UI, not Service Portal
questions.explanation: ATF exercises real platform interactions — form actions, list filters, REST calls, server-side asserts — and supports impersonation. It can be invoked during automated change validation. ATF complements (rather than replaces) developer-level unit tests, and it does support Service Portal page testing.

questions.question: Which feature lets a developer connect a scoped application to a Git repository for branch-based development?
options: Studio's Source Control integration
Update Set export
ATF
The Now CLI 'sn deploy' command
questions.explanation: Studio integrates with Git providers (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket) for source control — branch, commit, push, pull, stash, and apply remote changes. Update Sets capture changes but aren't versioned. ATF is for testing. There is no 'sn deploy' command in the now CLI today.

questions.question: An app committed to Git from a dev instance is pulled into a clone instance. Which artifact tracks the link between an in-instance app and its remote Git repo?
options: An entry in sys_scope showing the remote URL
A sys_repo_config (Repository Configuration) record
A property under 'glide.git.remote'
Nothing — the link is recreated by re-importing each time
questions.explanation: The Repository Configuration record (sys_repo_config and related) stores the remote URL, credentials, default branch, and current commit reference. sys_scope holds the app's identity and version, not its repo link.

questions.question: Which artifact is generated when you create a new application via Studio's 'Create from Application Repository' option?
options: A linked copy of the repository app, ready for customization within the boundaries the publisher allowed
A free-form unscoped duplicate of every artifact
A reference to the remote app — every change is sent upstream automatically
Nothing — you must export an Update Set first
questions.explanation: Repository-installed apps are linked clones with publisher-configured customization boundaries (e.g., protection policies). They are not freely duplicated, not auto-upstreamed, and do not require Update Sets.

questions.question: Which THREE are valid 'protection' options a publisher can set on an application artifact to control customization in installed instances?
options: None (fully editable)
Protected (admins can edit, others cannot)
Read-only (no one can edit, ever)
Permanently locked at the database level (sys_id immutable)
questions.explanation: Artifact protection supports None, Protected, and Read-only modes. There is no 'permanently locked at the database level' option — even Read-only is a platform-enforced policy that can be lifted only by republishing.

questions.question: An update set has 'Preview' errors after import. What is the CORRECT remediation order?
options: Commit the update set first to surface errors in production, then fix
Resolve each preview error (skip, accept remote, or accept local), then commit
Delete the update set and re-import
Ignore preview errors — they are advisory only
questions.explanation: Each preview error must be resolved — typically by accepting remote, accepting local (skipping), or merging — BEFORE committing. Committing first risks broken state; preview errors are not advisory; redoing imports rarely fixes the underlying conflict.

questions.question: Which command in the Now CLI installs a scoped application's dependencies and prepares the workspace for local development?
options: now-cli install
snc extension install
now-sdk apps install
There is no 'install dependencies' equivalent in the Now CLI / SDK toolchain
questions.explanation: The current Now CLI / Application SDK uses 'now-sdk apps install' to fetch app metadata and dependencies for local workspaces. The other commands are fabricated.

questions.question: What is the recommended way to roll back an application to a previous version after a problematic release?
options: Manually edit every record to its prior state
Use 'Rollback' from the Application Repository / installed app actions
Drop the application and reinstall from scratch
There is no rollback — only forward-fix is supported
questions.explanation: Repository-installed apps support a Rollback action that restores the previous version's metadata atomically. Manual edits are error-prone; full reinstall destroys customer customizations; rollback is explicitly supported.

questions.question: When debugging a Business Rule, which TWO platform tools are most useful?
options: Background Scripts to invoke the rule's logic ad hoc
The Script Debugger with breakpoints in the rule's script
gs.print() messages followed by checking syslog (System Logs > All)
Disabling all other Business Rules to isolate behavior
questions.explanation: The Script Debugger is the primary IDE-style tool; gs.print/gs.info logging is the classic backup. Background Scripts can help reproduce logic but run in their own context and don't capture rule events. Disabling unrelated rules is destructive and rarely necessary.

questions.question: Which statement BEST describes the difference between Update Sets and Application versioning?
options: Update Sets package incremental changes (often across global scope); Application versions are full app snapshots tied to a semantic version
They are identical — 'Update Set' is the old name for 'Application Version'
Update Sets are only for production deployment; versions are only for development
Application versions cannot be promoted across instances; Update Sets are the only delivery vehicle
questions.explanation: Update Sets capture deltas (especially in global scope) and are great for cross-instance promotion of customizations. Application versions are immutable snapshots of an entire scoped app, distributed via the repository. The two coexist and serve different lifecycles.

questions.question: Which platform record stores the metadata for a field on a table (type, max length, default value, attributes)?
options: sys_dictionary
sys_db_object
sys_choice
sys_metadata
questions.explanation: sys_dictionary holds one row per field per table. sys_db_object holds the table records themselves; sys_choice holds choice values; sys_metadata is the parent type all metadata extends.

questions.question: A developer needs to display a many-to-many relationship between Pet and Tag. Which approach is the ServiceNow-native pattern?
options: A Glide List field on Pet referencing Tag
Two Reference fields, one on each table
Duplicate the Tag table for each Pet
Use a Choice field with all possible tags
questions.explanation: Glide List is the platform's many-to-many field type — stored as a comma-separated list of sys_ids and rendered as a multi-pick list. Reference fields are 1-to-many on one side only.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a Document ID field?
options: Stores a polymorphic reference to ANY table (the table is chosen at runtime)
Stores a UUID for external systems
Stores the sys_id of the application
Stores a base64-encoded attachment
questions.explanation: Document ID is a polymorphic pointer that records both a table name and a sys_id, letting a single field reference rows on different tables (common in audit/activity logs).

questions.question: A 'Calculated' field is configured on a table. When is its value computed?
options: On every record insert/update, server-side, before save
Only when the user opens the form
Once per day via a scheduled job
Never automatically — only via UI Action
questions.explanation: Calculated fields run their server-side script automatically on insert/update before persistence, ensuring the stored value stays in sync with its inputs.

questions.question: Which dictionary attribute marks a field's value as encrypted at rest?
options: edge_encryption
password2
no_audit
read_only
questions.explanation: The 'password2' field type stores the value as one-way encrypted at the column level. Edge encryption is a separate proxy-based product; no_audit suppresses history; read_only is access, not storage.

questions.question: Studio's 'Schema Map' for a table primarily shows:
options: Parent table, extended-by tables, and related Reference fields
The physical storage layout on disk
Performance counters for queries on the table
The set of users with access
questions.explanation: Schema Map visualizes inheritance (parents/children) plus inbound/outbound References for the selected table — useful when planning data model changes.

questions.question: Which Studio panel lets you jump quickly between scripts (Business Rules, Script Includes, Client Scripts) inside the current app?
options: Code Search / Application Files navigator
Schema Map
Form Designer
Process Automation Designer
questions.explanation: Studio's Application Files / Code Search lets you filter and open any file by type or name across the entire app.

questions.question: A table is created with 'Extends' = NONE. Which field is automatically present on every record regardless?
options: sys_id, sys_created_on, sys_created_by, sys_updated_on, sys_updated_by
number (auto-number)
state and assignment_group
active
questions.explanation: Every table (extending or not) gets the sys_* audit/identity columns automatically. number, state, assignment_group, and active come only via Task inheritance or explicit configuration.

questions.question: Which TWO statements about choice fields are TRUE?
options: Choice values are stored in sys_choice, keyed by table and field
Choices can be made dependent on another field's value via the 'Dependent Value' configuration
Choices must be defined inline in the dictionary record and cannot be edited later
Adding a choice requires a database schema migration
questions.explanation: Choices live in sys_choice and support dependent values. They are editable at any time and require no schema migration — only metadata changes.

questions.question: What is the role of an 'Application Menu' record (sys_app_application)?
options: A category in the left navigator that groups Modules
The application's metadata identity
A permission scope for cross-scope access
A wrapper for the application's update sets
questions.explanation: Application Menus are navigator categories holding Modules. Application identity is sys_scope; cross-scope access uses sys_scope_privilege; update sets are sys_update_set.

questions.question: When extending a base table, in what order do Business Rules of type 'before' execute on insert?
options: Child table's BRs first, then parent's
Parent table's BRs first, then child's
Both run interleaved by their Order field, regardless of table
Only the child's BRs run — parent rules are skipped
questions.explanation: When evaluating BRs the platform considers all rules from the child and every ancestor and orders them by the 'Order' field — not by table position in the inheritance chain.

questions.question: An 'Application User' (sys_user) created during app install but not granted any role can still:
options: Read the home page and their own profile, nothing else by default
Modify any table — non-roled users have full CRUD by default
Approve sensitive financial records
Run server-side background scripts
questions.explanation: Without explicit roles or ACL grants, an authenticated user can see only their profile and base-system public content. Modification requires explicit role/ACL grants.

questions.question: A field needs a default value that depends on the current user's department. What is the right configuration?
options: Default Value: 'javascript:gs.getUser().getDepartmentID()'
Default Value: '${current_user.department}'
Add a UI Policy that sets the default
Add a display-time Business Rule that mutates the dictionary
questions.explanation: The dictionary's Default Value field supports the 'javascript:' prefix to evaluate a server-side script at insert time. The other syntaxes are not supported in this context.

questions.question: Which form personalization affects only the personalizing user, not the global form view?
options: Personalize Form (hamburger > Configure > Form Layout vs Personalize Form)
Form Designer changes
Dictionary 'default view'
Adding a Form Section
questions.explanation: 'Personalize Form' applies a per-user form preference. Configure > Form Layout / Form Designer / Form Section all change the global view for everyone.

questions.question: What is the recommended approach to localize a choice field's labels for multiple languages?
options: Store translations in sys_translated_text linked to sys_choice
Hard-code each translation in a Business Rule
Create a separate choice field per language
Localization is not supported for choice fields
questions.explanation: Choices are translated via sys_translated_text linked to the choice record. The platform looks up the current user's language and substitutes the translated label automatically.

questions.question: An 'application picker' shows in the top-left of Studio. What does it control?
options: Which application's scope subsequent edits will be saved into
Which user is impersonated
Which update set is current
Which language the platform uses
questions.explanation: The application picker selects the current scope context — every record created or modified is associated with the selected app/scope.

questions.question: Which TWO are valid uses of a 'Default fields list' on a table?
options: Specify which columns appear by default when the table is opened as a list
Reorder fields on the form view
Provide a fallback set of fields if a user has no personalization
Replace ACLs for field visibility
questions.explanation: Default Fields define the out-of-the-box list view columns and act as the fallback when no personalization exists. They don't affect form layout or substitute for ACLs.

questions.question: A dictionary entry's 'Calculation' field runs a script. Which variables are available inside the script?
options: current (the record), previous (prior values on update)
g_form and g_user
Only platform constants — no record access
Only HTTP request parameters
questions.explanation: Calculated field scripts receive 'current' (the record being saved) and 'previous' (prior values on updates), enabling expressions over either or both.

questions.question: An app's 'Logo' is configured. Where does it appear?
options: Module icons in the left navigator and the app card in Studio
On the Home page only
As the favicon for the entire instance
Replaces the user's profile picture
questions.explanation: App logos appear in the navigator menu next to the app's category and on the Studio app card. Favicons and home page branding are configured separately.

questions.question: An app contains 200 records of seed data (e.g., reference rows). What is the SUPPORTED way to ship that data with the app?
options: Create Fix Scripts that insert the data on install
Use 'Application Files' to package the records as Data Files
Reference the records via Update Set XML pasted into the source
Both A and B are acceptable depending on whether the data is install-once or per-version
questions.explanation: Fix Scripts run on install/upgrade and can populate or migrate data; Data Files in Application Files ship records directly. Both are first-class and the choice depends on whether logic is needed.

questions.question: Which TWO field attributes can be set on the dictionary entry to influence platform behavior?
options: no_audit (suppress history)
edge_encrypt (route through edge-encryption proxy)
ui_action (mark field as a button)
deprecated (warn on use)
questions.explanation: no_audit and edge_encrypt are real dictionary attributes. There is no ui_action or deprecated attribute on dictionary entries.

questions.question: A new view named 'mobile' is created for a table. How do you ensure the mobile app uses it?
options: Configure the mobile launcher / config to reference the 'mobile' view for that table
Rename the view to 'default' so it is automatically picked
Set the dictionary's 'Mobile View' attribute to true
Mobile always uses the default view; custom views are ignored
questions.explanation: The mobile experience explicitly maps tables to view names; you bind the view in the mobile config (or launcher/page config) to switch what's rendered.

questions.question: Which Studio capability lets a developer search the entire app for occurrences of a string (e.g., a field name)?
options: Code Search
Schema Map
ATF Test Runner
Update Set Preview
questions.explanation: Code Search scans across artifact bodies (scripts, conditions, queries) for matches — invaluable when renaming a field or hunting for callers.

questions.question: Two tables in different scopes need to share base fields. Which approach BEST preserves scope encapsulation while reducing duplication?
options: Create a parent table in the 'global' scope and extend it from both apps
Create a parent table in one app's scope and expose it via cross-scope read privileges, then extend from the other app
Hand-copy fields between the two tables and keep them in sync via a Business Rule
Use a Glide List on each table
questions.explanation: Parent-in-scoped-app with explicit cross-scope read privileges respects ownership and avoids global pollution. Putting the parent in global breaks isolation; manual sync diverges over time.

questions.question: Which client-script type fires when a user changes a cell value directly inside a list view?
options: onCellEdit
onChange
onSubmit
onLoad
questions.explanation: onCellEdit runs for inline list edits and receives a callback to allow/deny the edit. onChange fires on form fields only.

questions.question: g_form.addInfoMessage('Saved!') versus g_form.showFieldMsg('email', 'Bad format', 'error'). Which difference is correct?
options: addInfoMessage shows a banner at the top of the form; showFieldMsg attaches a message to a specific field
addInfoMessage is server-side; showFieldMsg is client-side
Both show banners — only the icon differs
showFieldMsg is deprecated
questions.explanation: addInfoMessage is a global form banner; showFieldMsg renders the message under a specific field. Both are client-side and current.

questions.question: A 'Form Annotation' is added between two fields. What does it provide?
options: Inline instructional text on the form (heading, line, or HTML content)
A new server-side calculation hook
Validation that runs on save
An attachment area
questions.explanation: Form Annotations are display-only elements (headings, separators, HTML text) inserted into the form layout to guide users — no logic attached.

questions.question: Which TWO UI Action 'Action condition' options allow a button to appear ONLY when the record meets some criteria?
options: The 'Condition' field (encoded query / JS expression)
The 'Show insert' / 'Show update' checkboxes
The 'Order' field
The 'Form button' checkbox
questions.explanation: Conditions and Show insert/update gate visibility. Order controls render order; Form button is a placement option.

questions.question: A developer wants to redirect the user to a different page when a UI Action runs. Which API call achieves this in a server-side UI Action script?
options: action.setRedirectURL('https://...')
g_form.redirect('https://...')
gs.redirect('https://...')
window.location.href = '...'
questions.explanation: Server-side UI Actions use action.setRedirectURL to navigate after save. g_form and window.location are client-only; gs.redirect doesn't exist.

questions.question: A onChange Client Script needs to fetch a related record's value. What is the BEST API for this in modern releases?
options: g_form.getReference('owner', callback)
Inline GlideRecord on the client (deprecated)
synchronous GlideAjax
Hard-code a Business Rule to push values
questions.explanation: g_form.getReference with a callback fetches the referenced record asynchronously and is the supported modern pattern. Inline client GlideRecord is deprecated; synchronous GlideAjax blocks the UI.

questions.question: Which g_form method conditionally locks a field so the user cannot edit it?
options: g_form.setReadOnly('field', true)
g_form.disable('field')
g_form.lock('field')
g_form.freeze('field')
questions.explanation: setReadOnly is the supported method. The others are not real APIs.

questions.question: A UI Page renders custom HTML at /<scope>/<page>.do. Which TWO components make up a UI Page?
options: HTML (Jelly) for layout
Client script (processing scripts) for client-side behavior
A Business Rule for server access
An ACL for inbound users
questions.explanation: UI Pages have HTML (Jelly) and a Client script section. Server logic typically lives in Script Includes called via GlideAjax. BRs and ACLs are independent platform constructs.

questions.question: What is a 'Formatter' on a form?
options: A reference to a UI Macro that renders custom HTML in a specific form position
A function that pretty-prints field values
A field type for currency
A type of related list
questions.explanation: Formatters are form-layout records that point to a UI Macro to embed rich custom content (e.g., contextual side panels) at a chosen position.

questions.question: How do you make a field read-only ONLY when the record's state is 'Closed'?
options: UI Policy with condition 'state = Closed' and action 'Read only = true'
Set the dictionary's Read Only attribute
Add an ACL with operation 'write' and a script that checks state
Either A or C, depending on whether the protection is UI-only or full
questions.explanation: UI Policy enforces UI-only read-only; an ACL also enforces it at API/import paths. Dictionary read-only is global. Both A and C are valid depending on threat model.

questions.question: Service Portal widget client controller and server script communicate via:
options: this.server.update() / this.server.get() and data/input bindings
g_form.submit()
RESTMessageV2
Direct GlideRecord on the client
questions.explanation: Widgets use the server.update / server.get pattern with 'data' (server-to-client) and 'input' (client-to-server) bindings. g_form is not available in widgets.

questions.question: A Service Portal page is built using:
options: A grid of rows, columns, and widgets
Only UI Macros
Only Angular components from an external CDN
Static HTML files served by the platform
questions.explanation: Service Portal pages are composed in the Page Designer by dropping widgets into rows/columns. Widgets may use Angular but the framework is hosted inside the platform.

questions.question: Which UI Builder concept REPLACES classic UI Pages in modern 'Next Experience' (Now Experience) apps?
options: Now Experience UI Framework components and pages built in UI Builder
Plain HTML attachments
Service Portal widgets only
UI Macros
questions.explanation: UI Builder composes Now Experience component pages — the strategic direction for new custom UIs. Classic UI Pages remain supported but not preferred.

questions.question: A form has 50 fields but only 10 should be visible to non-admins. The BEST control point is:
options: A UI Policy that hides fields for non-admin roles
Two separate views, one per audience
Hard-coding visibility in 50 Client Scripts
An ACL on each field with operation 'read'
questions.explanation: Two views are simpler, more maintainable, and align with the platform's intended pattern for role-based form simplification. UI Policies and ACLs are correct for finer-grained per-record logic.

questions.question: g_form.setMandatory('priority', true) is called in an onLoad Client Script. Which is TRUE?
options: priority becomes mandatory immediately; the user cannot save without filling it
Mandatory persists across the session for this user
Mandatory only applies after the next field change
setMandatory is not a real method
questions.explanation: setMandatory immediately marks the field, enforced on save. State applies to the current form lifecycle only.

questions.question: Which TWO are valid uses of g_form.getValue() vs g_form.getDisplayBox()?
options: getValue returns the stored value (sys_id for reference fields)
getDisplayBox returns the raw input element so additional UI tweaks can be applied
getValue throws on hidden fields
getDisplayBox is server-side
questions.explanation: getValue: stored value; getDisplayBox: DOM input for the field. Hidden fields still return values; both are client-side.

questions.question: When does an onLoad Client Script fire on a NEW record form (before save)?
options: When the user clicks 'New' and the platform renders the empty form
Only after the first save
Only when the user navigates via URL parameter
Never — onLoad only fires for existing records
questions.explanation: onLoad fires for new-record forms as well, immediately when the form renders — enabling default-value scripting or guidance UI.

questions.question: A Form Section is restricted to the role 'x_acme_pet.shop_owner'. Which behavior follows for a user without that role?
options: The section and its fields are hidden on the form
The fields appear read-only
The fields appear with greyed-out labels
Behavior is undefined
questions.explanation: Section-level role restriction hides the entire section (and its fields) from users without the role.

questions.question: A multi-row variable set (MRVS) on a catalog item is used to:
options: Collect multiple rows of structured input from a requester (e.g., a list of laptops)
Show a calendar widget
Display a chart
Restrict approval steps
questions.explanation: MRVS lets users add N rows of structured fields — typical for ordering multiple items with per-row attributes.

questions.question: What is the difference between a UI Page and a UI Macro?
options: UI Page is a full standalone page (its own URL); UI Macro is embedded inside another artifact
UI Page is server-only; UI Macro is client-only
UI Page is for admins; UI Macro is for users
There is no difference — they are aliases
questions.explanation: UI Pages render at their own URL; UI Macros are reusable Jelly snippets embedded in forms, lists, or other pages.

questions.question: A custom 'Save and Stay' UI Action wants to save the record but keep the user on the current form. What is the canonical pattern?
options: Set action.setRedirectURL(current.getLink(true)) so the platform returns to the same record
Use g_form.save() on the client and never run server logic
Use action.setReturnUrl(referer)
Disable the form's default Save
questions.explanation: Setting the redirect to current.getLink(true) returns to the saved record's form. g_form.save bypasses the UI Action; setReturnUrl is not the standard mechanism here.

questions.question: Which view is automatically used by a UI Action's link/button rendering when no specific view is configured?
options: The current form's view
Always 'default'
Always 'mobile'
Always 'list'
questions.explanation: UI Actions render in the context of the active view; explicit per-view configuration is optional.

questions.question: g_form.addOption('priority', '1', 'Critical') adds a choice value to a choice field. When does this added choice persist beyond the session?
options: Never — it is a runtime addition only
After the user saves the record
It is automatically written to sys_choice
After the next deployment
questions.explanation: addOption is a runtime DOM-level modification; the choice is gone after the form is closed. Persistent choices require sys_choice records.

questions.question: Which TWO modern alternatives exist to writing classic Client Scripts for form-level logic?
options: UI Policies for declarative show/hide/read-only/mandatory
Catalog Client Scripts for catalog items (alternate scope)
g_user.getRoles() in an onLoad script
Inline HTML in the form
questions.explanation: UI Policies are the no-code alternative for visibility/state; Catalog Client Scripts cover catalog-form interactions. The other options are syntactically still client scripts.

questions.question: What is a 'Subflow' in Flow Designer?
options: A reusable, parameterized sequence of actions callable from other flows or subflows
A snapshot of a flow's execution log
A deprecated form of workflow
An approval pattern only
questions.explanation: Subflows are reusable flows with inputs/outputs, called from other flows like functions.

questions.question: A flow's 'Action' fails with a runtime exception. Which TWO mechanisms allow the flow to handle it gracefully?
options: The action's 'Error Handler' branch
A 'Try / Catch' construct in Flow Designer
A Business Rule listening on the failure
Wrapping the flow in a Scheduled Job
questions.explanation: Actions expose Error Handlers; flows also support Try / Catch blocks. Business Rules and Scheduled Jobs are not error-handling primitives for flows.

questions.question: An event 'pet.adopted' is registered. To run code when the event fires, you create a:
options: Script Action subscribed to the event
Business Rule with the event name in the condition
UI Action with onclick eventQueue
ACL on the event table
questions.explanation: Script Actions are subscribed to a named event and run asynchronously when it is processed.

questions.question: Which Email Notification 'When to Send' option triggers based on an explicit gs.eventQueue call?
options: Event is fired
Record inserted or updated
Function
Scheduled
questions.explanation: 'Event is fired' lets the notification listen for a named event from sysevent — coupling delivery from detection.

questions.question: Which Flow Designer feature lets you make a decision based on a small lookup table of conditions and outputs?
options: Decision Table
Subflow
Action
Schedule
questions.explanation: Decision Tables encode multiple input/output rules and return the matching output — cleaner than nested If steps for simple lookups.

questions.question: A Notification's 'Recipients' tab supports which THREE selection types?
options: Users by Role
Static user list
All employees of customer X's parent company recursively (custom join)
By Group
questions.explanation: Users by Role, static user list, and Groups are first-class. Arbitrary cross-table joins require an Advanced/Scripted recipient.

questions.question: Workflow stages are most useful for:
options: Showing the user progress through a multi-step process (e.g., 'Approval > Build > Test')
Hiding errors in script blocks
Defining ACLs on the workflow record
Triggering integrations
questions.explanation: Stages provide a visual progress indicator on the form, mapped to milestones in a (classic) Workflow.

questions.question: Which TWO are true about asynchronous Business Rules?
options: They run in a separate transaction, after the user's update completes
They are best for non-urgent side effects (e.g., notifications, indexing)
They guarantee execution before the next form load
They run only when explicitly invoked by gs.execute()
questions.explanation: Async BRs decouple side effects; they have no ordering guarantee relative to the next page load and run automatically when the trigger event fires.

questions.question: A Flow Designer trigger is configured with 'Run as: System User'. What is the implication?
options: Actions run with elevated privileges, bypassing the triggering user's role checks for the flow's internal operations
The flow can only edit system tables
Notifications appear from 'System'
The flow runs only at 03:00
questions.explanation: Run as System runs the flow under the system user identity — useful when actions must affect tables the triggering user cannot edit. Use carefully to avoid privilege escalation surprises.

questions.question: Which API queues an event from a server-side script with two parameters available to subscribers?
options: gs.eventQueue('pet.adopted', current, 'critical', 'east-region')
gs.fire('pet.adopted', current)
current.event('pet.adopted', 'critical')
events.publish('pet.adopted')
questions.explanation: gs.eventQueue(name, record, parm1, parm2) is the standard signature. The others are not real platform APIs.

questions.question: A 'Catalog Task' approval needs to require both the requester's manager AND the asset owner. The cleanest mechanism is:
options: A Flow Designer 'Ask For Approval' step with multiple approvers (rule: 'All must approve')
Two separate Business Rules
Hard-coded approval emails
Reject by default; let approvers email back
questions.explanation: Ask For Approval supports composite rules (All / Any / By percentage), encapsulating the requirement declaratively.

questions.question: A Notification template uses $\{number\} and $\{caller_id.email\}. What does the dot-walking access?
options: Email of the user referenced by caller_id (a Reference field)
Email of the current record's creator
A literal field named 'caller_id.email' in the dictionary
An attachment named caller_id.email
questions.explanation: Notifications support dot-walking through Reference fields. caller_id.email = the email of the user the record's caller_id points to.

questions.question: Which TWO statements about Flow Designer's 'Run Asynchronously' option are TRUE?
options: The flow executes in a background queue, not in the user's transaction
The trigger does not block the user's save
It guarantees the flow finishes before the user sees the saved record
It is the only way to run flows
questions.explanation: Async flows decouple from user transactions, do not block, and obviously do not guarantee completion before the next render. Sync execution is also supported.

questions.question: An SLA Definition is used to:
options: Measure elapsed time against business hours for a record (e.g., time to resolution)
Authenticate API callers
Restrict access to certain views
Replace ACLs
questions.explanation: SLA Definitions calculate elapsed time against schedules and conditions and produce Task SLA records the platform tracks and reports on.

questions.question: A Subflow accepts an input 'recordRef' typed as 'Record'. What does typing it as 'Record' (versus 'String') buy you?
options: Type-aware actions (dot-walking, action inputs of matching type) inside the subflow
Faster execution
Encrypted storage
Automatic deduplication
questions.explanation: Typed inputs give Flow Designer type-aware data picker support — actions can use the record's fields and references seamlessly.

questions.question: An action authored as a 'Script Step' has access to which special object to read inputs and set outputs?
options: inputs and outputs (e.g., inputs.recordRef, outputs.result = ...)
current and previous
g_form and g_user
this.req and this.res
questions.explanation: Script Steps inside Flow Designer expose 'inputs' (declared input vars) and 'outputs' for setting return values, both flowing through the typed action contract.

questions.question: An Inbound Email Action stops processing further actions on the same email by:
options: Setting event.state = 'stop_processing' or via the action's 'Stop processing' checkbox
Returning false from the script
Calling email.abort()
Throwing an exception
questions.explanation: The 'Stop processing' flag (or equivalent in newer versions, setting state) terminates further inbound actions for this email message. Throwing or returning false is not the contract.

questions.question: A Scheduled Script Execution can run as a different user via:
options: The 'Run as' field set to a sys_user
The script switching gs.getUser()
A Business Rule before the job
It always runs as system
questions.explanation: Run as field elects an alternate user identity. The script cannot self-elevate by swapping gs.getUser(); the platform enforces identity at job start.

questions.question: A Transform Map's 'onBefore' script can:
options: Skip the current import row by setting ignore = true
Modify field values before write
Both A and B
Neither — onBefore is read-only
questions.explanation: onBefore scripts can mutate the target object and set ignore = true to skip the row entirely.

questions.question: An import is missing required fields on some rows. Which Transform Map behavior is configured by 'Run business rules' = false?
options: Disables target table Business Rules during the transform
Disables data policies on the target
Forces every row to insert
Skips validation entirely
questions.explanation: Disabling 'Run business rules' bypasses BRs on the target table — useful for bulk loads where rules would otherwise misfire or slow the import.

questions.question: Which TWO authentication types does RESTMessageV2 natively support without custom scripting?
options: Basic auth (user/password)
OAuth 2.0 (via Application Registry)
Kerberos via in-place Active Directory
DNS-only token rotation
questions.explanation: Basic and OAuth 2.0 (with several grant types) are first-class. Kerberos and ad-hoc DNS schemes are not built into the REST Message form.

questions.question: A Scripted REST API resource needs to read the request body. The correct API is:
options: request.body.data (or request.body.dataString)
current.body
g_form.getBody()
gs.getRequest().body
questions.explanation: Scripted REST resources receive a 'request' object exposing body.data and body.dataString. The other accessors are not part of the Scripted REST contract.

questions.question: An IntegrationHub 'Spoke' is:
options: A packaged set of Flow Designer actions for a specific third-party system (e.g., MS Teams)
A column in sys_import_set_row
A Scripted REST API namespace
A type of UI Action
questions.explanation: Spokes ship vendor-specific actions usable inside flows. Often they require an IntegrationHub entitlement.

questions.question: A Data Source is configured with type 'File' and Format 'JSON'. Where does the file come from?
options: An attachment on the Data Source record, an FTP/SFTP location, or a JDBC/URL retrieval
Always pulled from /etc/now/imports
Only via a UI upload at run time
Generated automatically by the platform
questions.explanation: Data Sources support multiple retrieval modes — attachment, network locations, MID-server-aided, etc.

questions.question: An outbound REST call must run through a MID Server for connectivity to an internal endpoint. Where is this configured?
options: On the REST Message record's 'Use MID Server' / 'MID Server' fields
In the calling Business Rule
On the Data Source
On the user's profile
questions.explanation: REST Messages specify whether (and which) MID server to route through — required when the target is internal-network only.

questions.question: Which TWO are valid coalesce strategies in Transform Maps?
options: Single-field coalesce (e.g., on email)
Multi-field composite coalesce (e.g., email + region)
Coalesce by sys_id only — never editable
Coalesce by reference qualifier
questions.explanation: Single-field and multi-field coalesce keys are supported. There is no 'reference-qualifier coalesce' and sys_id coalesce is not enforced as the only option.

questions.question: What does a Connection & Credential Alias enable?
options: Promoting an integration across instances without re-typing endpoints and secrets
Restricting which records a user can view
Bundling UI Policies
Replacing scoped ACLs
questions.explanation: Aliases decouple endpoint configuration from logic — integration code references the alias, and the alias points to the right connection/credential per environment.

questions.question: A REST request needs a custom HTTP header on every call. What is the right configuration?
options: Add the header to the REST Message's HTTP Headers related list (static), or set it per-call via setRequestHeader
Hard-code the header in a Business Rule that wraps the call
Configure it in the MID server only
Headers cannot be customized
questions.explanation: Static headers live on the REST Message; per-call dynamic headers use setRequestHeader on the request object.

questions.question: Which TWO are valid Scripted REST API versioning behaviors?
options: Mark a resource version 'Deprecated' (still callable, returns a header warning)
Mark a resource 'Default' so unversioned calls land on a specific version
Automatically translate v1 payloads to v2 schema
Force every caller to v0 silently
questions.explanation: Deprecated and Default flags are real behaviors. Auto schema translation is not built-in; silent v0 forcing is contrary to the model.

questions.question: What information does sys_import_set_row record?
options: One row of staging data plus the original source values and the transform outcome (insert/update/ignore/error)
An audit of role changes
A Workflow step
A flow execution log
questions.explanation: Each import-set row captures the raw input plus the result of each transform run — invaluable for troubleshooting bad imports.

questions.question: Which role provides 'elevated privileges' such as editing ACLs and high-security records, separate from admin?
options: security_admin
itil_admin
personalize_dictionary
elevated
questions.explanation: security_admin is the elevated role required for high-security actions like ACL edits, accepted only after explicit elevation in the UI.

questions.question: Domain separation primarily:
options: Partitions data so users in one domain cannot see records in another
Encrypts data at rest
Enables single sign-on
Provides row-level locking
questions.explanation: Domain separation is a multi-tenant data isolation feature used commonly by MSPs to keep customer data invisible across domains.

questions.question: A scoped app exposes a Scripted REST resource. By default, who can call it?
options: Only authenticated users that pass the resource's ACL
Anyone on the internet, anonymously
Only the app's developer
Only users with admin
questions.explanation: Scripted REST resources require authentication unless explicitly marked 'Requires authentication' false (and even then, an ACL still gates access).

questions.question: A 'Public Page' makes a UI Page accessible without login. Which TWO safeguards remain?
options: Captcha/anti-abuse controls if configured
ACLs on any data the page reads (must allow 'guest' or anonymous role)
It bypasses HTTPS
It elevates the caller to admin
questions.explanation: Public pages still respect ACLs (requiring grants to the guest/anonymous user), and admins can layer captchas. They do NOT bypass TLS or elevate anyone.

questions.question: A scoped app needs to invoke a function from another scoped app's Script Include. Which configuration is required?
options: The other app's Script Include must be marked 'Accessible from: All application scopes' OR have an explicit cross-scope read privilege
The calling user must have admin
The two apps must be in the same Update Set
Nothing — scoped scripts are always visible cross-scope
questions.explanation: Script Include 'Accessible from' on the target plus matching cross-scope privileges control cross-scope script reuse. User role doesn't override scoping; update sets are unrelated.

questions.question: What does setting an ACL's 'Active' flag to false accomplish?
options: Disables the ACL — it is ignored during access evaluation
Encrypts the records it covers
Hides the ACL from the UI but still evaluates it
Routes requests to a backup
questions.explanation: Inactive ACLs are skipped. There is no 'hidden but active' or routing semantics.

questions.question: ACLs are evaluated in which order for a single operation?
options: From the most specific (table.field) to the least specific (table.*); ALL applicable ACLs at the same level OR together, but levels are AND-ed
Random order with first-match-wins
Alphabetical by sys_id
By Order field only
questions.explanation: Multiple ACLs at the same level act as OR; different levels (table-level, field-level, *) are ANDed. Specificity is by table/field name pattern; Order influences within a level.

questions.question: Which THREE statements about 'Script' element in an ACL are TRUE?
options: It must set 'answer' to true or false to allow/deny
It runs in addition to the Role and Condition checks (all three must allow)
It can call gs APIs (gs.hasRole, gs.getUser, etc.)
It runs first and can short-circuit role checks
questions.explanation: ACL Script sets 'answer'; runs alongside role/condition (AND); has access to gs APIs. It does NOT short-circuit role checks.

questions.question: A 'Read-only mode' instance setting:
options: Disables writes platform-wide during upgrades
Encrypts every row
Forces every UI Action to disabled
Replaces ACLs
questions.explanation: Read-only mode (set during upgrades or by support) prevents writes globally so the underlying data isn't mutated during maintenance.

questions.question: Which 'Login policy' would prevent a user from logging in outside business hours?
options: Login Schedule Policy referencing a business-hours Schedule
An ACL on the user table
A Notification
A Service Portal page
questions.explanation: Login schedule policies tie authentication to a platform Schedule. ACLs gate access AFTER login; notifications and SP pages are unrelated to auth.

questions.question: When 'High Security Settings' plugin is active, which behaviour is enforced?
options: Many sensitive operations require security_admin elevation in addition to admin
Every record is encrypted
External calls are blocked
ACLs are disabled
questions.explanation: High Security adds the security_admin elevation gate for sensitive admin actions, and tightens default ACLs across several base tables.

questions.question: An 'Anonymous' user accessing a public page is internally mapped to which sys_user record?
options: sys_user record with user_name = 'guest' (the Guest user)
The application owner
Whatever user last logged in
A null user — no record
questions.explanation: Anonymous traffic runs under the 'guest' user. Grants to 'guest' determine what anonymous callers can see.

questions.question: Which TWO debugging aids help diagnose why an ACL is failing?
options: Enabling ACL Debug ('Session Debug') from the System Diagnostics menu
Inspecting the Application Log for security warnings
Disabling all ACLs and observing the difference
Looking at the Theme record
questions.explanation: Session Debug for ACLs renders the evaluation chain in the page footer; Application Logs capture related warnings. Disabling ACLs is destructive; themes are unrelated.

questions.question: A Reference field 'owner' points to sys_user. To prevent users from seeing the picker filled with inactive users, the best approach is:
options: Reference Qualifier: active=true
ACL on sys_user that hides inactive users platform-wide
Hide inactive users via a Business Rule
Filter via client script after the picker loads
questions.explanation: Reference Qualifiers are designed for picker filtering. Platform-wide ACLs are overkill and side-effect-y; BRs and client-side filtering after the fact are fragile.

questions.question: A scoped app reads from a base-system table (e.g., incident) that lives in 'global'. Which configuration is required?
options: Cross-scope read privilege from the scoped app to incident; the table's accessible-from setting must permit it
The user has the 'incident_user' role only
Nothing — base-system tables are always accessible
Re-host incident inside the scoped app
questions.explanation: Cross-scope privileges plus 'Accessible from' on the target table govern cross-scope reads. Base-system tables aren't automatically open.

questions.question: Field encryption 'Column-level encryption' protects data:
options: At rest in the database, transparent to scripts that read via Glide APIs
Only in transit to the client
Only inside notifications
Only when accessed by guests
questions.explanation: Column encryption encrypts the underlying storage; Glide APIs transparently decrypt for callers with appropriate roles. Encryption-in-transit is separate (TLS).

questions.question: What does 'Restricted Caller Access' control?
options: Whether (and which) other scoped applications can invoke this scope's scripts/tables
The user's calendar
The MID server's IP allowlist
Anonymous access to public pages
questions.explanation: Restricted Caller Access (RCA) is the platform mechanism through which a scoped app authorizes (or denies) cross-scope invocation requests — auditable in the sys_app_application form.

questions.question: Which TWO are recommended responses when an automated security scan finds a missing ACL on a custom table?
options: Add table.read / table.write / table.create / table.delete ACLs with appropriate roles and conditions
Add field-level ACLs for any sensitive columns
Disable the table to be safe
Mark the table 'Public' to standardize behavior
questions.explanation: Right answer: define explicit operation-level ACLs and field-level overlays for sensitive data. Disabling the table is overkill; marking 'Public' (no such switch in fact) goes the wrong direction.

questions.question: Studio's Git integration supports which TWO operations on a scoped app?
options: Create branch (and switch the in-instance app to it)
Commit + push changes to the remote
Auto-merge across unrelated scopes
Replace Update Sets entirely
