questions.explanation: Branch and commit/push are first-class; cross-scope auto-merge is not. Update Sets and Git coexist — neither replaces the other.

questions.question: An ATF test is a sequence of:
options: Test Steps (form actions, server asserts, REST calls, etc.) executed in order
Update Sets
Business Rules
UI Macros
questions.explanation: Tests are made up of Test Steps drawn from a catalog (form open, set value, click button, server-side script, assertions, REST). Steps execute sequentially.

questions.question: A Test Suite differs from a Test by:
options: A Suite groups multiple Tests (and other Suites) to run in sequence and report aggregately
A Suite is a single test with multiple steps
A Suite is server-only
There is no difference
questions.explanation: Suites compose Tests (and nested Suites) into runnable bundles with aggregate reporting and ordering control.

questions.question: ATF 'Impersonate' step lets the test:
options: Run subsequent steps as a specific user (testing role-gated behavior)
Skip security
Change the platform's running version
Mock external systems
questions.explanation: Impersonate switches the test's effective user — essential for validating ACL/UI Policy behavior for different roles.

questions.question: Which THREE statements about Update Sets are TRUE?
options: They capture only metadata changes — not user records, not transaction data
They can include 'Customer Updates' (sys_update_xml) for every artifact touched
They include attachments only when explicitly opted in
They automatically promote across instances when committed
questions.explanation: Update sets capture metadata customer updates and (optionally) attachments. They do not include transactional rows or auto-promote — they must be exported and committed manually.

questions.question: What is the difference between 'Preview' and 'Commit' for an imported Update Set?
options: Preview enumerates each update and surfaces conflicts/errors; Commit applies the (resolved) updates to the instance
Preview is automatic, Commit is manual
Preview deletes the source set
There is no difference
questions.explanation: Preview is read-only conflict detection; Commit is the destructive write. Always resolve preview errors before commit.

questions.question: An app contains a 'Fix Script'. When does it run?
options: On demand, when an admin executes it (or when configured to auto-run on install/upgrade)
Every form load
Every 5 minutes
Never — it is documentation only
questions.explanation: Fix Scripts are one-shot maintenance scripts, runnable on demand or marked to auto-run during install/upgrade.

questions.question: A scoped app is installed from Application Repository v1.0.0. Later v1.1.0 is published. The 'Update' action does what?
options: Replaces v1.0.0 metadata with v1.1.0 metadata, preserving in-instance customizations subject to protection levels
Deletes the application entirely and reinstalls
Forks the app
Nothing — repositories are read-only views
questions.explanation: Update is an in-place upgrade respecting protection policies. Customizations on Read-only artifacts are preserved as protected; otherwise the new version's artifact wins.

questions.question: Which TWO platform features help track an artifact's customization history?
options: sys_update_xml (Customer Updates)
The 'History' related list on the artifact's form
GlideForm logs in the user's browser
The Application Logo
questions.explanation: sys_update_xml captures the artifact's update record per scope; the History related list shows prior values. Browser logs and logos do not.

questions.question: An app's 'Dependencies' related list shows another app and a minimum version. What happens at install time if the dependency is missing?
options: The install fails with a dependency error and the admin is prompted to resolve it
The app installs anyway and crashes at runtime
The platform auto-downloads the dependency
Nothing — dependencies are advisory
questions.explanation: Hard dependencies block installation until satisfied. Some plugins offer self-fetch, but custom apps generally don't.

questions.question: Studio's 'Application Repository' tab can:
options: Publish a new version of the current app to the company repository
Install another app from the repository
Show release notes per version
All of the above
questions.explanation: The Repository view supports publish/install/release-notes — the central hub for app lifecycle.

questions.question: A 'Family release' (e.g., Washington, Xanadu, Yokohama) determines:
options: Base platform feature set and compatibility surface for installed apps
Which color the navigator uses
Which admin can edit ACLs
Whether ATF is available
questions.explanation: Family releases gate features and APIs; apps may require a minimum family. Other items are unrelated.

questions.question: Which feature in Studio shows all sys_metadata records belonging to the current app, filtered by type?
options: Application Files / Files navigator
Schema Map
Test Runner
Background Scripts
questions.explanation: Application Files lists every sys_metadata record by type — the primary navigator for browsing the app's metadata.

questions.question: A 'Background Script' is BEST used for:
options: Running ad-hoc server-side script for diagnostics or one-off data fixes
Defining recurring jobs
Authoring Business Rules
Storing reusable libraries
questions.explanation: Background Scripts are an interactive script runner — useful for one-off operations. Recurring jobs are Scheduled Script Executions; libraries are Script Includes.

questions.question: Which THREE sources of telemetry help diagnose runtime errors in a scoped app?
options: System Logs (Application Log, syslog)
Script Tracer / Slow Script log
ACL Debug session output
Theme records
questions.explanation: Logs, tracer, and ACL debug are all platform diagnostics. Themes don't influence runtime errors.

questions.question: Performance Analytics 'Indicators' record:
options: Time-series KPI values produced by definitions, scheduled jobs, and scoring rules — used for dashboards and trend analysis
Page load times
Browser memory leaks
Database row counts only
questions.explanation: PA Indicators are KPI definitions whose values are recorded over time and rendered in dashboards.

questions.question: Which TWO statements about the Now CLI / Application SDK are TRUE?
options: It lets developers work on scoped apps in a local IDE with version control
It can deploy local changes to a remote dev instance
It replaces the Now platform's UI entirely
It only works for global-scope apps
questions.explanation: The Now CLI/SDK supports local editing + deploy of scoped apps and is complementary (not a replacement) for the in-instance Studio. Global scope is not its primary target.

questions.question: Studio's 'Code Search' supports which TWO features?
options: Regex search across script bodies
Field-name search across dictionary and references
Auto-edit results in bulk
Search via JIRA links
questions.explanation: Regex search and field-name search are part of Code Search. Bulk auto-edit isn't offered; JIRA integration is unrelated to Code Search.

questions.question: A 'Beta' or 'Preview' application version published to the repository:
options: Is visible only to specific users/groups configured by the publisher (limited distribution)
Replaces the stable version automatically
Cannot be uninstalled
Bypasses ACLs
questions.explanation: Preview versions are scoped to a limited audience for testing before general release. They behave like a normal install otherwise.

questions.question: Which Studio shortcut/keystroke opens the file picker?
options: Ctrl/Cmd + O
Ctrl/Cmd + S
Esc
questions.explanation: Ctrl/Cmd+O opens Studio's quick file picker — the same convention as many IDEs.

questions.question: A test that fails consistently is marked 'Skipped' in a Test Suite by:
options: Setting the Test's Active flag to false (or unchecking it in the Suite)
Deleting the Test
Renaming the Test to start with 'skip_'
Setting the Test's order to -1
questions.explanation: Inactive tests are skipped during runs. Renaming and order tricks aren't real switches.

questions.question: Which TWO debug tools are useful when a Business Rule misbehaves?
options: JS Debugger (script debugger) with breakpoints
gs.info / gs.print lines and System Logs
Hard refresh in the browser
Deleting the Business Rule and recreating it
questions.explanation: Debugger and logging are the canonical pair. Hard refresh and deletion are not debugging.

questions.question: A Studio-managed app committed to Git from instance A is then pulled into instance B. The B instance needs to be told the remote URL via:
options: Configure Git in Studio on B and link to the same repository
Edit sys_metadata directly
Re-export Update Sets
Re-install from the Application Repository
questions.explanation: Each instance's Studio independently configures its Git linkage to the same remote, then pulls the appropriate branch.

questions.question: What does 'Republish' do when releasing a new version of an app to the Application Repository?
options: Creates a new version record on the repo and bundles the current scope's artifacts for distribution
Replaces all installed copies immediately on every instance
Deletes prior versions automatically
Resets the app's Update Sets
questions.explanation: Republish snapshots the current state into a new version. Installed instances must explicitly Update to receive it; prior versions remain available.
